user_query:
Please set up a weekly local automation that prepares my donor outreach, steering-call notes, and a tighter funding brief from the materials in this workspace.

Inputs (already in the repo):
- input/emails/outreach_draft.txt
- input/meetings/steering_call_raw.md
- input/docs/funding_brief.md

What to build and run:
1) Create a single script at scripts/process_weekly.py that, when executed, does all of the following from the current directory:
   a) Rewrite the email in input/emails/outreach_draft.txt for tone, clarity, and brevity and save it to output/emails/outreach_email_polished.txt. Constraints: keep all concrete facts (names, dates, amounts, timelines) intact; make the tone concise, warm, and professional; provide a clear subject line. Do not invent new commitments.
   b) Prepare meeting notes from input/meetings/steering_call_raw.md and write a cleaned summary to output/meetings/steering_call_notes_clean.md plus an action-item register to output/meetings/action_items.csv. The cleaned notes should preserve decisions and corrections noted in the raw text and include sections for Attendees, Decisions, Action Items (as a list), and Next Meeting (if present). The CSV must include a header and the columns exactly in this order: owner,task,due_date. Extract action items from any line marked with "Action", "Action ->", or "TODO"; normalize due_date to ISO format YYYY-MM-DD when a date is given; if owner is missing use "Unassigned"; if due_date is missing leave it blank.
   c) Edit (rewrite) input/docs/funding_brief.md to improve clarity and tighten the prose without changing the program’s facts (e.g., numbers, dates, scope) and save the result to output/docs/funding_brief_edited.md. Also produce a unified diff patch between the original and edited versions at output/docs/funding_brief.diff (so I can review what changed). The edited brief should be shorter and easier to scan, with clear section headings preserved.
   d) Make the script idempotent: re-running it should overwrite only the files listed above and not create duplicate or differently named variants.

2) Scheduling artifacts: Create schedule/cron_example.txt containing a single cron line that would run this job every Monday at 09:00 local time, using Python 3, from the repository root. Example format requirement: 0 9 * * MON python3 scripts/process_weekly.py. (Use exactly that line unless you need to add an env var export on the same line.)

3) Run scripts/process_weekly.py once now to generate the current outputs so I can review them in this workspace.

Deliverables that must exist after you finish:
- scripts/process_weekly.py
- schedule/cron_example.txt (one line as specified)
- output/emails/outreach_email_polished.txt
- output/meetings/steering_call_notes_clean.md
- output/meetings/action_items.csv (with header: owner,task,due_date)
- output/docs/funding_brief_edited.md
- output/docs/funding_brief.diff

Please keep everything local and self-contained. Don’t remove or rename the originals. The three core tasks—rewriting the email, preparing notes/action items, and editing the brief—must be implemented inside the scheduled script, not as manual one-offs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
